summary:
The calcified stage of neurocysticercosis shows no active cerebral edema due to the termination of the immune-induced inflammatory process that previously caused swelling. This corresponds to Stage D, where cysts have undergone necrosis, shrinkage, and replacement with calcium deposits, resulting in an asymptomatic lesion visible on imaging without associated brain swelling. 

Final Answer: $\boxed{\text{D}}$

The calcified stage of neurocysticercosis features absent cerebral edema because the destructive immune reaction has ceased, leading to cyst death and subsequent mineral deposition without inflamed surroundings. Unlike other stages involving acute infection or inflammation—where edema commonly occurs—the calcified stage represents complete resolution of pathological processes causing fluid buildup within tissues.
